reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Using 'fabs' from <math.h> on integer character values is unnecessary and incorrect; also, when i=0, a[i-1] accesses invalid memory (a[-1]).]

So if the string has a valid lowercase expansion (first 'if' true), it prints the expansion, then checks uppercase (false), then checks digit (false), and then executes this 'else', printing an extra '-'. This leads to duplicate output. For example, input "a-d" would output "bcd-", which is wrong.
